text:
Once upon a time in a forest filled with singing birds and sparkling streams, there was a wise old owl named Orion. He had witnessed countless seasons and knew every bird by name.

Each morning, Orion would perched on a low branch, scanning the woods for the first robin of spring. One day, he heard a melody unlike anything he had ever heard. It was a flute-like tune, carried on the breeze.

Curious, Orion flew down to investigate. There was a young girl sitting by a clearing. Her flowing hair shimmered like moonlight, and flowers were woven into her dress. She was tapping her foot to an invisible rhythm.

Orion listened to her play an ancient song. It was a tale of love lost, a quest for a lost star, and the magic it held. She sang it every Spring — a promise made long ago.

The villagers had never heard it before. They gathered, amazed.

"It's ancient! It's lovely!" whispered a villager. Orion
